title: Lieberkuhna piloselloides
intro: Lieberkuhna piloselloides, also known as False Hawk's Beard, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. The bioactive constituents of Lieberkuhna piloselloides include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Lieberkuhna piloselloides is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Lieberkuhna piloselloides is a species of flowering plant in the family Compositae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Lieberkuhna, which is a group of flowering plants. The species is further classified as a member of the tribe Lactuceae, a subgroup of the Compositae family. Lieberkuhna piloselloides is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. The classification of Lieberkuhna piloselloides reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the Compositae family.
